title: Jim Ovia and the Lagos property market
description: Jim Ovia and the Lagos property market  TheCable
content: Stay connected via Google News Founder of Zenith Bank, Jim Ovia, is expanding his business portfolio into the luxury end of Nigeria’s real estate business, betting that high-end property offers stronger returns than banking. Not many would be convinced by that, but coming from a man who founded the bank 35 years ago and grew it into one of the biggest lenders in the country, the statement captured attention across sectors. Ovia announced his retirement from banking as chairman of Zenith last month. His company, Quantum Luxury Properties, is developing two major high-end residential towers in Lagos: the first, Metropolitan Towers, is a 26-floor high-rise where each apartment would sell for $1.85 million; and the second, Quantum Luxury Towers, with 44 apartments, each starting from a $2.8 million selling price. Two decades ago, Ovia had entered the property business with the development of high-end event centres in Victoria Island. The success of The Civic Centre on the highbrow Ozumba Mbadiwe Road in Victoria Island certainly prepared the 74-year-old for a life in retirement. The Lagos luxury property market is one of the biggest and most profitable in Africa. According to intelligence firm, Estate Intel, about 135 homes in Lagos are currently priced above $1 million each. The market is projected to grow substantially over the next five years, with nearly 1,400 additional luxury homes projected to enter the market by 2029. The Akwa Ibom state government is adding 32 new apartments to the expected 1,400. Located in Victoria Island and developed by the state government, Ibom Towers is an 18-storey investment that will feature 10 one-bedroom units, 10 two-bedroom units, nine three-bedroom units, and three two-bedroom penthouses and service quarters. I attended the turning of the sod event at the construction site in October 2024. Construction has been progressing steadily since. Advertisement Ibom Towers is designed as an income-generating venture for the state, whose IGR has been among the least in the country. Construction started last year and will be completed in 2027. At the current market rate, the government expects to earn over $70 million (about N105 billion) in gross income from the venture. The initial construction cost for the 18-storey Ibom Towers was N50 billion. But an upward review is expected to make up for rising costs. Even if the final cost of the project is N80 billion (my estimation), the state could end up with between N20 billion and N30 billion as net profit – enough to fund some of the administration’s many social and economic programmes. The government is also planning other property investments in Abuja. Gov. Umo Eno says the main objective is to widen the government’s revenue streams beyond FAAC allocations. Internally generated revenue currently stands at about N100 billion per year, up from N40 billion per year in 2023. The increase is due to plugging of leakages and theft with the introduction of the Treasury Single Ac
summary: Jim Ovia's entry into the luxury real estate market in Lagos is a significant development, potentially shifting the market's focus towards high-end properties and increasing competition for premium segments.

mixta_relevance.direct_impact: This development may impact our strategic priority of shifting new-sales mix toward higher-margin / faster-converting segments, as it introduces a new competitor in the premium segment.
mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: It may also affect our pricing strategy and costs, as well as the regulatory environment, due to increased competition and potential changes in market dynamics.
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: This creates an opportunity for Mixta Africa to differentiate itself and maintain its market share in the premium segment by focusing on unique selling points and high-quality products.
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: There is a risk that Mixta Africa may struggle to compete with Jim Ovia's luxury properties, potentially threatening our execution and revenue targets.

title: FAAC shares N2.3 trillion May 2026 revenue as statutory earnings rise
description:
The Federation Account Allocation Committee (FAAC) has distributed a total of N2.3 trillion to the Federal Government, state governments and local government councils as revenue allocation for May 2026.

The post FAAC shares N2.3 trillion May 2026 revenue as statutory earnings rise appeared first on Nairametrics.
content:
The Federation Account Allocation Committee (FAAC) has distributed a total of N2.3 trillion to the Federal Government, state governments and local government councils as revenue allocation for May 2026.

The post FAAC shares N2.3 trillion May 2026 revenue as statutory earnings rise appeared first on Nairametrics.
summary: The recent FAAC revenue allocation of N2.3 trillion for May 2026 is a positive indicator for the Lagos real estate market, as it suggests increased government spending and potential infrastructure development, which can boost demand for premium and commercial properties.

mixta_relevance.direct_impact: This revenue allocation can boost demand for our Lakowe Lakes premium products and LNT commercial plots, aligning with our strategic priority of shifting new-sales mix toward higher-margin segments.
mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: Increased government spending can lead to improved infrastructure in Lagos, reducing costs and enhancing the overall business environment, which can benefit our operations.
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: The increased revenue allocation presents an opportunity for Mixta Africa to pursue institutional/bulk transactions and corporate mortgage programmes, as it can attract more investors and developers to the Lagos market.
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: However, the increased government spending also poses a risk of inflation, which can impact our costs and pricing, particularly for our affordable housing segment.

title: Gov Mbah: Tinubu’s Increased Allocation to States Driving Enugu Infrastructure Growth
description: • President has not ignored any section of Nigeria, says Onanuga Emmanuel Addeh in Abuja Governor Peter Mbah of Enugu State has said increased financial support to subnational governments under
content:
• President has not ignored any section of Nigeria, says Onanuga

Emmanuel Addeh in Abuja

Governor Peter Mbah of Enugu State has said increased financial support to subnational governments under the administration of President Bola Tinubu has been responsible for the remarkable infrastructure development in the state.

Mbah made the revelation on Tuesday when he received members of the Renewed Hope Ambassadors on a media tour of the South-east. The team was jointly led by Bayo Onanuga, Special Adviser to the President on Information and Strategy and Sunday Dare, the Special Adviser on Media and Public Communication.

A statement by the Director of Media & Publicity, Renewed Hope Ambassadors, Tunde Rahman, yesterday stated that the the media tour, which aims to showcase key federal and state government projects across the South-east geopolitical zone, is the initiative of the National Coordinator of the Renewed Hope Ambassadors, Governor Hope Uzodimma of Imo State.

The governor commended Tinubu’s interventions, particularly in security, education, healthcare, agriculture, and road infrastructure, describing them as critical enablers of the state’s ambitious development agenda.

He said: “It would have been impossible, to say the least, for us as a state to have done things at a scale we did without some of the bold and courageous policies of Mr President. First of all, what the policies of Mr President did for subnationals is largely to free up resources.

“So, I think there is a clear intention. I think Mr President has been very intentional about how this trillion-dollar economy is to be achieved.  He strongly believes this won’t come from the top down; it ‘ll come from the periphery. To achieve that, you have to strengthen the periphery both in terms of responsibility and in terms of providing them with the finances.

“I think if you just cast your mind back, even having a conversation with my predecessor, he would tell you what they went through. Sometimes, they struggled from the moment they returned from FAAC to pay salaries; immediately after salaries were paid, nothing was left for capital expenditure.

“So, what we are seeing in Enugu today and at the scale these things are happening is only possible because we now have more funds coming from the Federation Account and there is quite a lot of support from the centre to the subnationals and for us is to make good use of that opportunity and that’s essentially what we’re doing.”

The governor noted that the improved fiscal environment created by the federal government has enabled Enugu State to accelerate the implementation of transformative projects aimed at improving citizens’ quality of life and stimulating economic growth.

“So, whether you want to look at it from the removal of the subsidies, the unification of the exchange rates, the forex, if you look at the reform, the tax sector, and you know, quite a whole lot of great initiatives. If you look at the NELFund policy, this is something we’re excited about because, typically, the list of scholarships you get, the applications, and the applicants you get every year to fund are almost unimaginable.

“But today we encourage them, our students, to access NELFund, and that again frees up funds for us to do other things. So the impact is invaluable, and we thank Mr President for all that,” he said.

On security, the state’s investment in security architecture, he said, has strengthened intelligence gathering, surveillance systems, and rapid response mechanisms across urban and rural communities, thereby creating a conducive environment for businesses and investments to thrive.

The governor also showcased the ongoing construction of the state-of-the-art Enugu International Specialist Hospital, which is expected to provide advanced medical services, reduce medical tourism, and strengthen healthcare delivery within the state and beyond.

Onanuga, who spoke after inspecting several ongoing and completed federal and state government projects across Enugu State, commended the governor for his developmental strides. He noted that the collaboration between the federal government and state governments is producing visible results that are directly impacting the lives of Nigerians.

“President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has not ignored any section of Nigeria. Everywhere has been taken care of. And as the engineer from the project site just explained to us, people are going to feel the impact of the road work going on in many parts of the country,” he said.

The projects inspected included the ongoing construction of the Flyover Interchange Bridge at Abakpa Junction in Enugu, the ongoing reconstruction of the 38.6-kilometre Enugu–Onitsha–Port Harcourt concrete pavement road, and the dualisation of the 21.5-kilometre Enugu International Airport Road extending to the Ebonyi State border.

The team also inspected the ongoing dualisation of a 40-kilometre Owo–Ubahu–Amankanu–Umualo–Ekem road, the Enugu State Tractors Assembling Plant, among others.
summary: Increased allocation to states by President Tinubu may boost infrastructure development in Enugu, potentially driving demand for premium and commercial properties in Lagos, particularly in the Lekki corridor.

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: Increased infrastructure development in Enugu may lead to a shift in demand for premium and commercial properties in Lagos, potentially benefiting Mixta Africa's Lakowe Lakes and LNT projects.
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: The increased allocation to states may create opportunities for Mixta Africa to partner with state governments on infrastructure development projects, enhancing its credibility and competitiveness in the market.

title: The private space race is spurring a luxury hotel land grab for Florida oceanfront property
description: The rise of the private space industry, with companies like SpaceX and Blue Origin, has spurred demand for commercial real estate on Florida's space coast.
content: The rise of the private space industry, with companies like SpaceX and Blue Origin, has spurred demand for commercial real estate on Florida's space coast.
summary: The private space race's impact on Florida's luxury hotel market has negligible direct implications for Lagos' real estate market, but it highlights the potential for innovative industries to drive demand for premium properties, which could inform Mixta Africa's strategy for its high-end developments like Lakowe Lakes. This trend may also influence the company's approach to attracting international investors and partners. However, the immediate effects on Lagos' market are likely to be minimal.

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: Potential influence on strategy for high-end developments and attracting international investors
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: Informing approach to premium property development and international partnerships

title: Mortgage rates are now falling but demand is still weaker
description: A mixed week for mortgage rates resulted in less demand from both current homeowners and potential homebuyers.
content: A mixed week for mortgage rates resulted in less demand from both current homeowners and potential homebuyers.
summary: Falling mortgage rates may boost demand for premium and mid-market segments, but weaker demand could impact Mixta Africa's revenue targets, particularly in the affordable housing sector.

mixta_relevance.direct_impact: Weaker demand may impact our revenue targets, particularly in the affordable housing sector, as we focus on shifting new-sales mix toward higher-margin segments.
mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: Falling mortgage rates may influence pricing and costs, but the impact on our competitors and regulatory environment is uncertain.
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: The trend of falling mortgage rates presents an opportunity to attract more premium and mid-market buyers, aligning with our strategic priorities.
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: Weaker demand poses a risk to our execution, particularly in the affordable housing segment, if we fail to adapt to changing market conditions.

title: Bill limiting investors from buying homes set to speed through Congress
description: Top lawmakers reached an agreement on a key housing bill that would limit investor ownership, clearing the way for passage through both chambers of Congress.
content: An affordable housing bill limiting how many single-family homes major investors can buy is poised to be signed into law before the end of the month after key lawmakers in the House and Senate reached an agreement Tuesday. The bill, which is focused on increasing the supply of homes, would not include a controversial provision requiring major investors to sell any housing units they build within seven years, but would cap the number of single-family homes they could buy at 350. Senate Majority Leader John Thune, R-S.D., told reporters on Tuesday that the bill could clear the Senate as soon as this week, with an initial vote to begin to advance the measure on Thursday evening. Thune said he hoped the House could take up the bill when it returns next week. Previous versions of the bill have cleared the House with strong levels of support, meaning the House could use an expedited process to get the legislation done. Sen. Elizabeth Warren, D-Mass., who has helped helm the bill as the top Democrat on the Senate committee overseeing housing, said the bill is important not only because of the focus on affordability, but what it means for how Congress handles private equity. “Never before has Congress put any restriction on the ability of private equity to move into whatever industry they want, buy up whatever they want and destroy whatever they want,” she told CNBC in a short Capitol hallway interview. “This bill is historic because it puts a big fat ‘no’ right in front of private equity’s growth as it tries to mow through our neighborhoods.” This site is now part of Versant. summary: A new US housing bill limiting major investors to buying 350 single-family homes may have a ripple effect on the Lagos real estate market, particularly in the affordable housing segment, as it could lead to increased demand for Nigerian properties.

mixta_relevance.direct_impact: This bill may not have a direct impact on our strategic priorities, as our focus is on premium Lakowe Lakes products and LNT commercial plots. However, it could indirectly affect our pricing and costs, as increased demand for Nigerian properties may drive up prices.
mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: The bill may lead to increased demand for Nigerian properties, which could drive up prices and affect our costs. Additionally, it may create a strategic opportunity for us to tap into the diaspora buyer channel via the 'Own It 4 Sure' (O4S) S.U.R.E. framework.
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: The bill creates an opportunity for us to tap into the diaspora buyer channel and potentially increase our sales in the affordable housing segment.
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: The bill may not pose a significant risk to our execution, but it could lead to increased competition in the market, which may affect our pricing and costs.

title: Developers shift to commercial property as State dominates housing
description: Developers shift to commercial property as State dominates housing  Business Daily
content: Developers shift to commercial property as State dominates housing  Business Daily
summary: The shift of developers to commercial property as the State dominates housing may lead to increased competition in the commercial sector, potentially affecting Mixta Africa's strategic priorities and active projects. This trend could also impact the pricing and demand for residential properties in Lagos. As a result, Mixta Africa may need to reassess its sales strategy and focus on higher-margin products to remain competitive.
trending_topics: commercial property, housing market, real estate trends

mixta_relevance.direct_impact: May affect the sales of Mixta Africa's commercial plots in Lagos New Town and the shift in focus towards higher-margin products
mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: Could impact the pricing and demand for residential properties in Lagos, affecting Mixta Africa's revenue targets
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: Potential to capitalize on the growing demand for commercial properties and increase market share
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: Increased competition in the commercial sector may threaten Mixta Africa's execution and revenue targets

title: Barclays explains why weak China consumer data may not trigger policy support
description: Jian Chang, Barclays’ Chief China Economist, breaks down China’s economic data for May. She attributes the decline in retail sales to a deepening slump in the property sector, as well as base effects from the 618 shopping festival in 2025. She explains why Chinese policymakers are not likely to undertake more policy support despite the weak consumer data.
content: Jian Chang, Barclays’ Chief China Economist, breaks down China’s economic data for May. She attributes the decline in retail sales to a deepening slump in the property sector, as well as base effects from the 618 shopping festival in 2025. She explains why Chinese policymakers are not likely to undertake more policy support despite the weak consumer data.
summary: Weak China consumer data may not trigger policy support, potentially limiting global demand for premium and mid-market properties, which could impact Mixta Africa's sales mix and revenue targets, particularly for Lakowe Lakes and LNT commercial plots.

mixta_relevance.direct_impact: This may not directly affect our strategic priorities, but it could influence our sales mix and revenue targets, particularly for Lakowe Lakes and LNT commercial plots.
mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: The global economic trends may impact pricing and costs, but this is not a direct impact on Mixta Africa.
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: This creates an opportunity for Mixta Africa to focus on affordable housing and mid-market segments, which may be less affected by global economic trends.
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: This does not threaten execution, but it may require Mixta Africa to adapt its sales strategy and revenue projections.

Please note, your choice through the toggle is specific to this site, for this browser and device. If you access other Versant sites and services, you will need to opt out for each.
summary: The drop in homebuilder sentiment in June may lead to a decrease in new launches and construction activities in the Lagos real estate market, potentially affecting the supply of housing units and impacting Mixta Africa's strategic priorities. This could also influence the pricing strategy for Mixta Africa's active projects, such as Lakowe Crossings and Lagos New Town. As a result, the company may need to reassess its revenue targets and sales mix to mitigate potential risks.

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: Potential decrease in demand for housing units, affecting pricing strategy and sales mix
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: Potential risk to revenue targets and project timelines

title: These stocks pay attractive dividends and offer 'turmoil insurance'
description: Investors looking for stability in times of volatility often turn to dividend stocks — including real estate investment trusts. Here is one analyst's top picks.
content: Investors looking for stability in times of volatility often turn to dividend stocks — including real estate investment trusts. Here is one analyst's top picks.
summary: The article highlights dividend-paying real estate stocks as a stable investment option, which may attract investors to the Lagos real estate market, potentially increasing demand for premium and commercial properties, and benefiting Mixta Africa's strategic priorities.

mixta_relevance.direct_impact: This may increase demand for premium and commercial properties, aligning with Mixta Africa's strategic priorities to shift new-sales mix toward higher-margin segments.
mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: The increased demand may lead to higher pricing and costs for Mixta Africa's projects, particularly in Lakowe Lakes and Lagos New Town (LNT).
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: The article's focus on dividend-paying stocks may create an opportunity for Mixta Africa to rebrand its premium products and highlight their dividend-generating potential.
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: The increased demand may also lead to higher competition from other developers, potentially threatening Mixta Africa's market share.

title: Commercial real estate saw record lending competition in April, according to JLL
description: Global credit activity among lenders as well as overall competitiveness of loan terms reached an all-time high in April, JLL found.
content: Global credit activity among lenders as well as overall competitiveness of loan terms reached an all-time high in April, JLL found.
summary: Record lending competition in April is a positive indicator for Lagos real estate market, potentially driving down borrowing costs and increasing access to capital for developers, including Mixta Africa. This could benefit our strategic priorities, particularly in pursuing institutional/bulk transactions and corporate mortgage programmes. However, it may also lead to increased competition for premium and commercial segments.

mixta_relevance.direct_impact: This could benefit our pursuit of institutional/bulk transactions and corporate mortgage programmes, particularly with the MREIF/MOFI diaspora and domestic mortgage partnership with Access Bank.
mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: Increased competition for premium and commercial segments may impact pricing and costs, while also affecting our competitors' strategies.
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: Record lending competition presents an opportunity for Mixta Africa to scale Lagos New Town (LNT) and pursue institutional transactions, potentially driving growth and revenue.

title: Jim Ovia Shifts To Property Development, Says Real Estate More Profitable Than Banking
description: Jim Ovia Shifts To Property Development, Says Real Estate More Profitable Than Banking  Arise News
content: Jim Ovia, the founder and largest individual shareholder of Zenith Bank Plc, says he is turning his attention fully to real estate after stepping down as chairman of the bank, arguing that the property sector offers better returns than banking. The 74-year-old businessman is expanding his activities through Quantum Luxury Properties Ltd., where he is developing two high-end residential projects in Lagos. The developments include the 26-storey Metropolitan Towers, where units start from $1.85 million, and the 44-unit Quantum Luxury Towers, with apartments priced from $2.8 million. Speaking from his office at Civic Towers in Lagos, Ovia said both projects were attracting strong interest from buyers and had already recorded significant sales. “Real estate is more profitable than banking,” Ovia said. “I’m moving into real estate full-time,” he added. Related News: Jim Ovia Named Doyen of Nigerian Banking Industry At The NGX Closing Gong Ceremony Shettima Applauds Zenith Bank At 35th Anniversary, Hails Jim Ovia’s Role In Banking Technology Mustafa Bello Emerges Chairman Of Zenith Bank After Jim Ovia Retires His decision comes as Lagos’ luxury property market continues to attract wealthy investors seeking protection from inflation, currency depreciation and limited investment alternatives. According to estate surveying firm Diya Fatimilehin & Co., rental prices in Lagos’ luxury districts of Banana Island, Ikoyi and Victoria Island rose by 51% between 2024 and 2025, reinforcing demand for premium real estate. Jubril Enakele, Chief Executive Officer of IRON Capital Partners and a former executive within Zenith’s investment banking operations, said Ovia’s interest in the sector predates his latest move. “Mr. Ovia’s interest in real estate started much earlier than just now,” Enakele said. He added that Ovia’s focus on design, infrastructure and asset quality had long been evident in projects associated with the banker. Ovia stepped down as chairman of Zenith Bank on May 5 after completing the maximum 12-year tenure allowed by regulators. While he remains the bank’s largest individual shareholder, his departure marks the end of a leadership era at the institution he founded in 1990 with an initial capital base of about ₦20 million. Under his leadership, Zenith Bank grew into Nigeria’s largest lender by market value and one of the country’s most profitable financial institutions. The bank recorded profits exceeding ₦1 trillion in both 2024 and 2025. Beyond banking, Ovia has built a portfolio of investments that includes Civic Centre and Civic Towers in Lagos, the sale of Visafone to MTN Group, and an early investment in Moniepoint. His latest move shows growing confidence in Nigeria’s property market, where rising construction costs, rapid urbanisation and a housing deficit estimated at more than 20 million units continue to support demand across both affordable and premium housing segments. For Ovia, the next phase of his business career appears firmly roo
summary: The entry of Jim Ovia into the Lagos real estate market with high-end residential projects is expected to increase competition in the premium segment, potentially affecting Mixta Africa's sales and pricing strategy for its luxury products. This development may also lead to a shift in buyer preferences towards high-end properties, impacting the overall market dynamics. As a result, Mixta Africa may need to reassess its marketing and sales approach to remain competitive.

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: Potential impact on sales and pricing strategy for luxury products, such as Lakowe Lakes
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: Opportunity to learn from Jim Ovia's high-end residential projects and incorporate similar features or marketing strategies into Mixta Africa's own luxury developments
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: Increased competition in the premium segment may pose a risk to Mixta Africa's market share and revenue targets
